info.title: Computer Science Student (Year 2)
info.heroLead: Computer Science Student (Year 2) — web development, cybersecurity and concrete projects.
info.about: I am a computer science student passionate about creating modern, high-performance web applications and cybersecurity. I love solving complex problems and constantly learning new technologies.
home_paragraphs: Cybersecurity is part of my journey: awareness of best practices, curiosity for analysis and monitoring. This site brings together my profile, my skills and projects that I am proud to present.
I am open to discussions about an internship, a student project or a collaboration — browse the 'About', 'Portfolio' and 'Contact' pages to learn more.

about.title: ABOUT
about.intro: This page gathers my personal information, technical skills and educational background.

cyber.title: Cybersecurity Focus
cyber.note: Constantly monitoring for new vulnerabilities (CVE) and attack techniques.

portfolio.title: MY
portfolio.intro: Projects realized: web applications, exercises and recent achievements.

sportmates.title: Sportmates
sportmates.desc: Sports community platform with admin dashboard (user management, posts and real-time statistics).

ecommerce.title: E-commerce Project
ecommerce.desc: Online sales web application with cart, authentication and payment. Project realized in a team of 3 people.

weather.title: Weather App
weather.desc: Weather app using OpenWeatherMap API with geolocation and 7-day forecast.

todo.title: Task Manager
todo.desc: Task management application with drag & drop, categories and local synchronization.

sort.title: Sorting Algorithms
sort.desc: Sorting algorithm visualizer (bubble sort, quicksort, merge sort) with animations.

l2.title: Computer Science Bachelor (Year 2)
l2.desc: In-depth study of software development, networks and security.

cyber_f.title: Cybersecurity Training
cyber_f.desc: Introduction to offensive and defensive security fundamentals.

web_f.title: Web Development Training
web_f.desc: Web projects and front/back best practices.

contact.title: GET IN
contact.intro: For an internship, a project or a question — leave me a message.
contact.lead: Feel free to reach out. I am open to discussing projects, ideas, or internship and collaboration opportunities.
